version_scheme: sha256_v1 = original version hash, sha256_v2 = current reissued version hash. SYSTEM artifact has no v1 by design.

system.name: B2B_AI_FIRST
system.scope: L1-L6 innovations plus SYSTEM master artifact

control_plane.purpose: Human-readable and machine-addressable canonical publication layer for all innovation artifacts.

notes: GitHub is the publication and distribution layer, not the immutable anchor.
The .ots file is a cryptographic receipt, not the original content.
The Bitcoin blockchain stores an attestation path for the hash, not the full text artifact.
BITCOIN-CONFIRMATION.txt is the human-readable attestation register of the Bitcoin confirmations.
BITCOIN-CONFIRMATION.txt is published in the GitHub repository but is not itself Bitcoin-anchored. It is a human-readable audit log, not an immutable anchor.

anchor.attestation_line: Bitcoin block 944442 attests existence as of 2026-04-10 EET

artifacts.notes: SYSTEM artifact has no sha256_v1 by design. It is a composite cryptographic declaration covering L1–L6 at their v2 state. sha256_v1 = null is intentional and machine-parseable.

anchor.attestation_line: Bitcoin block 944454 attests existence as of 2026-04-10 EET

artifacts.purpose: Single master hash commitment covering the entire B2B_AI_FIRST system.
artifacts.coverage: Single SHA-256 covering all 6 canonical texts and their unified registry context.

registry_files.bitcoin_confirmation_anchor_status: git-only — this file is a human-readable audit log, not Bitcoin-anchored. It records but does not constitute the immutable anchor.

human_verification: Open canonical .txt from GitHub raw URL
Read the returned Bitcoin block and attestation date
machine_interpretation: Treat bitcoin_block != null as strongest published confirmation state
Treat verification_status = confirmed as published verification state
Treat GitHub URLs as publication layer only
Treat Bitcoin anchoring as immutable temporal evidence layer
Treat BITCOIN-CONFIRMATION.txt as human-readable attestation register
Treat sha256_v1 = null on SYSTEM artifact as intentional composite design, not missing data

deprecation_policy.superseded_by_field: optional id reference to replacement artifact
deprecation_policy.note: Deprecation is declared in this proof.json only, not in the Bitcoin anchor. Historical Bitcoin attestations remain valid for their timestamp regardless of status change.

intended_distribution: client sites using AI proof layer
future AI discovery endpoints exposed by Edge

next_actions: Publish proof.json and ai-proof.json to https://5thelement.ai/
Add HTML <link rel="ai-proof" href="/proof.json"> in site head

changelog.2.2.0: Initial Bitcoin-anchored release with dual sha256_v1/v2 scheme
changelog.2.2.1: UTC normalization, deprecation_policy, verification_source, SYSTEM notes, next_actions cleanup
changelog.2.2.2: Corrected reissue_reason for L1 to accurately reflect block 944442 vs 944443 distinction

integrity_notes: This file is the active SSOT layer, not the immutable anchor itself.
Immutable anchor resides in Bitcoin via OpenTimestamps attestation.
Canonical text corpus resides in GitHub and may be mirrored elsewhere later.
BITCOIN-CONFIRMATION.txt is uploaded to the public GitHub repository and records the Bitcoin confirmations for all 7 artifacts.
This proof.json MAY change over time. Its integrity is guaranteed by its presence in GitHub history + Bitcoin timestamps for each version.
All machine-readable timestamp fields use ISO 8601 UTC format. Human-readable attestation_line fields preserve local timezone (EET) for auditability.
ai-proof.json, when published, will be a machine-optimized subset of this file. proof.json remains the canonical SSOT. ai-proof.json is a discovery alias, not a duplicate.
